meta.name: Forgotten Industries
meta.tagline: An archive and evidence-based memoir that explores what happens to the things we leave behind; abandoned places, unfinished projects, deserted machines, and the parts of ourselves we once thought lost.
meta.voice: intimate, precise, scientific, medical, archival, technical, emotionally honest

projects.title: CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal Restoration
projects.working_title: Rebuilding and Archiving a CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal 2014 Time Capsule
projects.summary: Documentation and restoration of a white powder-coated CaseLabs Mercury S8 with pedestal, stored for roughly a decade and returned to the bench as part hardware archive, part recovery log, part proof of life.

origin.context: The project paused after Matthew's father died and returned in 2026 as part of rebuilding life, hobbies, and identity.

layout_goals: Clean serial loop before experimenting with complexity
Functioning proof-of-concept system before any sale decision

route: Down through midplate to pedestal via QDCs
preferences: Barbs and clamps over excessive angled fittings
Hardline or copper possible later as aesthetic evolution

proof_of_concept.thesis: The restored Mercury S8 plus pedestal may be more valuable as a complete, functioning showcase system than as a collection of separate parts. The rare value is the chassis, restored CaseLabs hardware, Aquaero ecosystem, dual-D5 infrastructure, QDC-integrated pedestal loop, documentation, and Forgotten Industries provenance; CPU and GPU hardware can be replaced by a future owner.
build_direction: Use any functioning CPU/platform already available in storage
Use any functioning GPU or GPU set already available in storage
Fully assemble and fill the cooling loop
Verify pumps, Aquaero, sensors, QDC path, and pedestal integration
Run stress tests and document stable operation
time_capsule_option: GTX 780 Ti, Titan, GTX 970, or GTX 980 Ti era graphics hardware
EK waterblocks and period-correct loop hardware
Aquaero 6 XT, dual D5 pumps, and Noiseblocker eLoops
proof_of_concept.decision_rule: Complete inventory and chassis restoration before selling any restoration-critical component. Do not decide whether to sell the ship while it is still in dry dock.

documentation_style: Mark unknown parts temporarily with removable labels
Write logs as observations, not just progress updates

projects.title: Garage Archaeology
projects.summary: Recovered objects, parts, machines, tools, and forgotten projects treated as artifacts. The record begins before the object is cleaned or assigned a final meaning.
documentation_style: Record storage state, condition, missing hardware, and first hypotheses

projects.title: Field Notes
projects.summary: Process notes, experiments, methods, failures, findings, and design decisions for technical recovery through craft.
documentation_style: Use methods, observations, and findings when they help
Keep reflection grounded in objects and actions
Let the technical documentation carry the emotion

projects.title: PEREGRINE Drone Experiments
projects.status: active / A01 grounded / A02 lost / A03 planned
projects.summary: Small aircraft experimentation and recovery dossier covering Perry 1 / PEREGRINE-A01 as the DJI Mini 4K awning recovery and troubleshooting bird, Perry 2 / PEREGRINE-A02 as the DJI Mini 3 lost after a 3 AM roof incident, and Perry 3 / PEREGRINE-A03 as the planned Mini 3 replacement aircraft. The archive preserves flight logs, crash reports, maintenance notes, recovery routes, error codes, safety calls, and uncertain details as evidence.
documentation_style: Preserve flight conditions, error codes, and recovery route before interpretation
Treat calibration as secondary until physical damage is inspected
Record uncertain aircraft identity and compatibility details as NEEDS_CONFIRMATION
Photograph arms, propellers, motors, battery bay, gimbal, and error screens before repair

projects.title: REDEEMER NZXT H440 Watercooled Build
projects.summary: Instagram-recovered build dossier for REDEEMER, the Forgotten Industries white NZXT H440 mid-tower custom liquid-cooled PC. The recovered evidence documents the March-May 2016 build sequence, including component staging, watercooling assembly, leak testing, custom cabling, Windows install, HUE+ lighting, and final gameplay testing.
documentation_style: Treat Instagram captions as primary evidence
Preserve exact timestamps and source post links
Separate direct REDEEMER mentions from build-adjacent support posts
Keep component list tied to the recovered May 8, 2016 post

inventory.name: CaseLabs Mercury S8
inventory.status: core archive object
inventory.notes: White powder-coated Mercury S8. First photo mapping now ties broad chassis, tray, rear-panel, and table-layout evidence to this core object. Continue identifying panels, fasteners, window configuration, and any missing hardware before cleaning.

inventory.name: CaseLabs pedestal
inventory.status: core restoration object
inventory.notes: Pedestal planned for radiator capacity, QDC pass-throughs, and service drain. Confirm exact accessory configuration before layout decisions.

inventory.name: EK XTX 360 radiator
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and leak test
inventory.status: candidate for restored loop
inventory.notes: Heavy cooling overhead from the original watercooling plan. First photo mapping shows used EK radiators with visible fin dust/debris and fan assemblies. Needs flush, exterior cleaning, thread inspection, fin check, and leak test.

inventory.name: EK 360 slim radiator
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and leak test
inventory.status: needs inspection
inventory.notes: Thin 360 radiator present in the recovered cooling inventory. Confirm exact model and fitment.

inventory.name: Noiseblocker eLoop white PWM fans
inventory.status: inventory / possible reuse or sale
inventory.notes: Test bearing noise, PWM response, cable condition, and visual yellowing. Decide later which units remain with the archive.

inventory.name: Aqua Computer D5 Aquabus pumps
inventory.status: candidate for restored loop
inventory.notes: Intended for a dual-pump layout with Aquaero control. Photo closeups confirm Aqua Computer Aquabus markings and show paired pump/fitting context. Test before any loop planning becomes final.

inventory.name: DDC pumps with EK clear tops
inventory.status: needs inspection
inventory.notes: Identify exact pump variants and top condition. Record unknowns rather than guessing.

inventory.name: Aquaero 6 XT
inventory.status: candidate for restored loop
inventory.notes: Controller for pumps, fans, and sensors. First photo mapping shows the display, front controls, and exposed PCB/header side. Confirm display, buttons, headers, and Aquabus behavior.

inventory.name: Large EK cylinder reservoir
inventory.status: candidate for restored loop
inventory.notes: Intended to remain visible through the right-side window. Photo mapping now includes initial stills plus the 2026-06-05 detail rotation from the Splunking intake. Inspect acrylic, threads, brackets, and O-rings.

inventory.name: Corsair AX1500i power supply
inventory.condition: stored in plastic bin / needs cable inventory and electrical test
inventory.status: physical custody evidence / needs testing
inventory.notes: Physical AX1500i label documented in the initial photo batch. Keep separate from the Instagram-documented REDEEMER AX1200i record until cable set, serial, modular leads, and electrical status are confirmed.

inventory.name: EK-MOSFET MSI X99 XPower water block
inventory.status: inventory / needs board compatibility confirmation
inventory.notes: Box label identifies EK-MOSFET MSI X99 XPower. Confirm whether the block, thermal pads, screws, manual, and any seals are present before assigning value or compatibility.

inventory.name: Corsair radiator-mounted 120mm fans
inventory.model: 120mm radiator fan / exact model pending label check
inventory.status: physical custody evidence / needs count and model confirmation
inventory.notes: Visible fan labels and center caps identify Corsair fans, separate from the remembered Noiseblocker eLoop set. Record as a separate fan group until every radiator/fan assembly is counted and labels are photographed.

inventory.name: Quick-disconnect fitting pair
inventory.model: quick-disconnect pair / likely G1/4 liquid-cooling fittings
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and O-ring inspection
inventory.status: needs manufacturer identification and leak test
inventory.notes: Pair photographed beside the Aqua Computer pump detail shots with ruler scale. Identify manufacturer and thread type before reuse; inspect internal seals and perform a leak test.

inventory.name: CaseLabs white window and vent panel set
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and fitment sorting
inventory.status: temporary grouped panel record / needs exact position mapping
inventory.notes: White CaseLabs panels with window cutouts, ventilation mesh, and fastener holes. This is a temporary grouped record: next pass should map each panel to exact chassis side, orientation, window/mesh configuration, and missing fasteners.

inventory.name: CaseLabs white frame and rail set
inventory.condition: used / needs sorting and hardware check
inventory.status: temporary grouped frame record / needs fitment confirmation
inventory.notes: White structural frames/rails photographed in both the initial batch and the Splunking intake. Treat as a temporary grouped record until each rail/frame is laid against the chassis and assigned a stable position.

inventory.name: CaseLabs motherboard tray and midplate assembly
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and alignment check
inventory.status: temporary grouped internal-panel record
inventory.notes: White internal CaseLabs tray/midplate pieces with pass-through openings, fan/vent sections, and visible wear. Confirm exact S8 position, standoff layout, pass-through grommets, and missing fasteners.

inventory.name: CaseLabs small white accessory plate set
inventory.status: temporary grouped small-parts record
inventory.notes: Small white plates, covers, brackets, and hardware bags from the Splunking station pass. Several pieces repeat from multiple angles; do not assume count from photo count. Assign individual temporary IDs during the next bench sort.

inventory.name: CaseLabs white mesh/filter insert group
inventory.condition: used / visible dust and handling marks
inventory.status: temporary grouped filter record / needs exact fitment mapping
inventory.notes: Perforated/mesh white CaseLabs inserts photographed separately during Splunking. Treat as a temporary filter/panel subgroup until exact chassis position, orientation, and retained hardware are confirmed.

inventory.name: REDEEMER NZXT H440 white mid-tower

inventory.notes: REDEEMER chassis identified by the formal May 8, 2016 Instagram component list and earlier H440 radiator-fitment posts. Record as evidence-backed, not as confirmed present in storage.

inventory.status: recovered Instagram evidence / needs physical confirmation
inventory.notes: Instagram caption identifies a pair of Intel 535 480GB SSDs for REDEEMER, intended for RAID0 as the main drive. Preserve as a paired record unless later physical evidence separates them.

inventory.name: REDEEMER Corsair AX1200i power supply

inventory.notes: Caption states the AX1200i was repurposed from the test bench for REDEEMER. Later intake should confirm whether this PSU is still in the archive, in a different machine, or gone.

inventory.name: REDEEMER NZXT HUE+ lighting controller
inventory.status: recovered Instagram evidence / needs physical confirmation

inventory.name: REDEEMER EK-Supremacy EVO CPU block
inventory.status: recovered Instagram evidence / needs physical confirmation
inventory.notes: CPU block is documented before REDEEMER is named and then appears in the formal REDEEMER spec. Confirm whether the block stayed with the Z170 platform after teardown.

inventory.name: REDEEMER EK-RAM Monarch memory cooling assembly

inventory.name: REDEEMER EK-FC980 GTX Ti Classy KPE GPU block

inventory.model: EK-FC980 GTX Ti Classy KPE Backplate Nickel

inventory.name: REDEEMER EK-Ekoolant Pastel White
inventory.condition: Instagram-documented / consumed or physical custody unknown
inventory.status: recovered Instagram evidence / consumable record
inventory.notes: Loop-fill post identifies EK-Ekoolant Pastel White and records an 8+ hour no-leak run. Treat as consumable evidence rather than expected surviving inventory.

inventory.name: REDEEMER Mainframe Customs Teleios white cable set
inventory.status: recovered Instagram evidence / needs physical confirmation
inventory.notes: Captions document Mainframe Customs cables for REDEEMER and a Teleios White detail shot. Earlier April 19 caption mentions ordered extensions, so confirm whether this was a full PSU set, extensions, or both.

inventory.name: REDEEMER 65-inch curved Samsung test display
inventory.status: context object for final REDEEMER test
inventory.notes: May 17, 2016 test post places REDEEMER on a 65-inch curved Samsung display. This is a context object rather than a core PC component, but it anchors the final recovered test evidence.

inventory.name: Perry 1 / PEREGRINE-A01
inventory.status: grounded pending arm, motor, propeller, and ESC/power diagnostics
inventory.notes: First PEREGRINE aircraft. A01 got stuck on the awning, was recovered, and then became the troubleshooting bird. Known concerns include Error 30210, back-right arm damage, motor resistance, propeller mismatch possibility, and overtightening risk. Treat as grounded until physical inspection clears the arm, motor, propellers, screws, wiring, and ESC/power path.

inventory.name: Perry 2 / PEREGRINE-A02
inventory.condition: lost / likely on roof / not recovered
inventory.status: no unsafe roof recovery; replace before risking a climb
inventory.notes: Newer PEREGRINE platform with DJI RC. Corrected archive identity: A02 is the DJI Mini 3, not the Mini 3 Pro. After the A01 recovery and new flight confidence, A02 was flown at roughly 3 AM and lost, described in the field record as "yeeted into the abyss." The likely location is the roof. Recovery is explicitly deferred: replacing the aircraft is preferable to an unsafe high-roof climb.

inventory.name: Perry 3 / PEREGRINE-A03
inventory.status: target platform for next PEREGRINE aircraft
inventory.notes: Planned Mini 3 replacement aircraft for Perry 3. Track acquisition, baseline inspection, firmware, battery inventory, propeller package, and first flight as separate archive events once the aircraft is in hand.

inventory.name: Initial local archive photo batch
inventory.condition: preserved local files / mixed JPEG, MOV, and PNG sources
inventory.status: raw photographic evidence / needs object-level mapping
inventory.notes: Local first-pass photo asset batch currently present in the local archive working tree. Matthew referenced this body as the first 150-photo set; the checked-in local folder currently contains 129 files: 126 JPEG, 2 MOV, and 1 PNG. Contact-sheet review of rendered stills shows CaseLabs chassis and panel evidence, radiator and fan hardware, filters, trays, packaging, cable and component context, room/process context, and the Forgotten Industries identity asset. See docs/intake/2026-06-05-initial-photos.md, docs/intake/2026-06-05-initial-photos-files.csv, and docs/intake/2026-06-05-initial-photos.sha256 before renaming, moving, or deriving public assets.

inventory.name: 2026-06-05 Splunking raw photo intake
inventory.status: raw photographic evidence / pending curated previews and object-level mapping
inventory.notes: Raw iCloud Splunking intake copied into the local repository on 2026-06-05. Contains 80 files: IMG_1080.HEIC through IMG_1159.HEIC, with 79 HEIC files and 1 PNG totaling 423,137,971 bytes. Originals are preserved untouched for custody and later object-level mapping. The current environment did not produce trustworthy HEIC visual previews, so identification should happen from native viewing tools or curated derivatives. See docs/intake/2026-06-05-splunking.md, docs/intake/2026-06-05-splunking-files.csv, and docs/intake/2026-06-05-splunking.sha256.

fieldLogs.title: Rebuilding and Archiving a CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal 2014 Time Capsule

sections.heading: Condition Found
sections.body: White powder-coated chassis and pedestal recovered after roughly a decade in storage. The object should be photographed as found before dust, missing fasteners, or provisional assemblies are disturbed.

sections.heading: Observations
sections.body: The case appears to be the core archive object. Some STH-10 accessories may be present. Watercooling parts include EK 360 radiators, Aqua Computer D5 Aquabus pumps, DDC pumps with clear tops, an Aquaero 6 XT, a large EK cylinder reservoir, and approximately twenty or more white Noiseblocker eLoop PWM fans.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.body: Identify panel by panel. Assign temporary removable labels to unknown parts. Separate fasteners by location. Photograph each assembly before cleaning. Record uncertainty directly instead of smoothing it out.

sections.heading: Restoration Notes
sections.body: Start with a serviceable serial loop using EPDM tubing, barbs, and clamps. Keep the pedestal radiator path and quick-disconnect pass-throughs accessible. Function comes first; copper or hardline can become an aesthetic evolution later.

sections.heading: What It Means / Reflection
sections.body: This is not only a computer case. It is an interrupted map. The restoration matters because it turns private discontinuity into visible evidence: the hands still know how to build.

sections.heading: Next Actions
sections.body: Complete photo pass. Confirm exact panel configuration. Leak-test radiators. Test fans and pumps. Build the inventory record before deciding what stays, what sells, and what remains archive only.

sections.heading: Principle
sections.body: Recovery starts with the object as found. Dust, missing hardware, bad assumptions, and provisional assemblies are part of the record.

sections.heading: Method
sections.body: Photograph the object before cleaning. Label uncertainty. Record what is visible, what is missing, and what cannot yet be known.

sections.heading: Finding
sections.body: The technical record protects the emotional truth from performance. Evidence first. Interpretation later.

fieldLogs.title: PEREGRINE-A01 Crash Recovery Intake
fieldLogs.system: DJI Mini 4K aircraft / crash recovery and repair diagnostics

sections.heading: Condition Found
sections.body: PEREGRINE-A01 is recorded as the DJI Mini 4K crash/recovery bird. The incident chain for A01 is awning first: the aircraft got stuck on the awning, was recovered, and then became the troubleshooting bird. Known damage notes include back-right arm damage and abnormal motor resistance.

sections.heading: Observations
sections.body: Error 30210 is present in the working diagnostic tree. Treat the error as a hardware-adjacent warning until the aircraft has been physically inspected. Propeller mismatch and overtightened propeller screws remain active concerns.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.body: Photograph the aircraft before repair. Capture close photos of each arm, motor, propeller pair, screw, battery bay, gimbal, shell seam, and app error screen. Compare motor free-spin by corner. Keep removed propellers and screws grouped by motor position.

sections.heading: Restoration Notes
sections.body: Calibration should be secondary to physical inspection. DJI guidance for Mini-series propellers emphasizes correct frame-arm placement, same-package propeller pairing, replacement screws where specified, and avoiding mixed propeller types or packages.

sections.heading: What It Means / Reflection
sections.body: PEREGRINE belongs in the archive because it is a field system, not a toy record. The flight, the crash, the recovery route, the repair uncertainty, and the next launch all become part of the machine's evidence trail.

sections.heading: Next Actions
sections.body: Ground A01. Complete photo intake. Confirm exact propeller sets and screw condition. Inspect the back-right arm and motor for resistance, wiring damage, shell deformation, and ESC/power symptoms. Create the A02 loss record separately. Track Perry 3 / PEREGRINE-A03 as a planned Mini 3 replacement before acquisition.

fieldLogs.title: PEREGRINE-A02 Loss / No Roof Recovery Rule
fieldLogs.system: DJI Mini 3 aircraft / loss record and recovery decision
fieldLogs.status: lost / likely roof / recovery deferred for safety

sections.heading: Condition Found
sections.body: PEREGRINE-A02 is recorded as the DJI Mini 3 platform with DJI RC. After recovering A01 and gaining confidence with new flight skills, A02 was flown at roughly 3 AM and lost. The working field description is "yeeted into the abyss."

sections.heading: Observations
sections.body: The aircraft is believed to be on the roof, but that location remains unconfirmed. The important record is not only where the aircraft may be; it is the decision not to create a second incident by climbing a high roof for a replaceable machine.

sections.heading: Recovery Decision
sections.body: No unsafe roof recovery. No nighttime roof recovery. No solo climb. Replacement cost is preferable to a fall risk. A future recovery can be reconsidered only with safe access, daylight, stable equipment, and a reason stronger than saving the aircraft.

sections.heading: What It Means / Reflection
sections.body: Confidence is part of the log too. A01 taught recovery and repair; A02 marks the boundary where field work becomes risk management. The archive preserves the loss without demanding a dangerous retrieval.

sections.heading: Next Actions
sections.body: Treat A02 as lost unless recovered under safe conditions. Preserve the A02 visual mark and loss record. Track Perry 3 / PEREGRINE-A03 as the planned Mini 3 replacement, with acquisition, baseline inspection, and first flight logged separately.

fieldLogs.title: Mercury S8 Restoration Proof-of-Concept Build Strategy
fieldLogs.status: strategic note / inventory before sale decision

sections.heading: Strategic Realization
sections.body: The Mercury S8 plus pedestal restoration may have more value as a completed, functioning showcase system than as a collection of parts. The CPU and GPU are not the value center. The rare value is the complete Mercury S8 plus pedestal, restored CaseLabs hardware, functional Aquaero ecosystem, dual-D5 infrastructure, QDC-integrated pedestal loop, documentation, provenance, and Forgotten Industries restoration story.

sections.heading: Proof-of-Concept Build
sections.body: Use any functioning CPU platform and GPU hardware already available in storage. Assemble the cooling loop, verify pumps, verify the Aquaero, verify sensors, confirm the QDC architecture, confirm pedestal integration, and run stress tests. The proof statement is simple: this machine works today, and the internals can be upgraded tomorrow.

sections.heading: Time Capsule Option
sections.body: A period-correct enthusiast build may carry collector appeal even if it is not performance-optimal by modern standards. Candidate hardware includes X79 or X99 platforms, GTX 780 Ti, Titan, GTX 970, or GTX 980 Ti era graphics hardware, EK blocks, Aquaero 6 XT, dual D5 pumps, and Noiseblocker eLoop fans. The appeal is preservation of a golden-era custom watercooling system, not benchmark superiority.

sections.heading: Inventory Pattern
sections.body: The restoration has already shown a repeated pattern: a missing component is feared, inventory continues, and the component or an equivalent support part is later discovered. Examples include radiators, fan inventory, pump inventory, Aquaero hardware, and mounting hardware. Do not assume parts are missing until the complete inventory is finished.

sections.heading: Financial Reality
sections.body: Money is needed now, but selling restoration-critical components before inventory is complete may be shortsighted. The immediate pressure is real; the archive still needs sequence discipline. The hard-to-replace value is the restored chassis and watercooling infrastructure, not any single commodity compute component.

sections.heading: Decision Order
sections.body: Complete inventory. Complete chassis restoration. Verify the infrastructure. Evaluate market value. Then decide whether to keep it, archive-build it, modern-build it, or sell it as a complete system. Do not decide whether to sell the ship while it is still in dry dock.

sections.heading: Next Actions
sections.body: Continue garage inventory before liquidation decisions. Account for remaining GPUs, platforms, fittings, blocks, cables, fasteners, and watercooling support parts. Treat the proof-of-concept build as a validation path: pumps, Aquaero, sensors, QDCs, radiators, pedestal, leak testing, thermal testing, and documented stable operation.
